title: Prevent Unauthorized and Unintended Information Transfer
description: Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.

checks.name: DLP Agent is Present and Running
checks.purpose: Verify a Data Loss Prevention agent (Microsoft Defender for Endpoint / Purview) is running to monitor and prevent unauthorized data transfer

checks.name: USB/Removable Storage is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify the USBSTOR service Start value is 4 (disabled), preventing removable storage from being used as an unauthorized information transfer channel

checks.name: Outbound Firewall Block Rules Exist
checks.purpose: Confirm outbound firewall Block rules are configured to restrict unauthorized data exfiltration paths

checks.name: DLP Agent is Present and Running
checks.purpose: Verify a DLP agent is running on the server to detect and prevent unauthorized data transfers

checks.name: USB/Removable Storage is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify removable storage is disabled via the USBSTOR registry key on the server to prevent physical data exfiltration

checks.name: Outbound Firewall Block Rules Exist
checks.purpose: Confirm outbound firewall Block rules restrict unauthorized data exfiltration channels from the server

checks.name: Information Transfer Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify a documented policy controls authorized information transfer channels and prohibits unauthorized sharing — policy review only

checks.name: USB Storage Module is Blacklisted
checks.purpose: Verify the usb-storage kernel module is blacklisted in /etc/modprobe.d/ or not currently loaded, preventing removable storage as a data transfer channel

checks.name: Outbound Firewall Rules Are Configured
checks.purpose: Verify iptables OUTPUT or firewalld rules are configured to restrict unauthorized outbound data transfer paths

checks.name: DLP or Auditing Solution Present
checks.purpose: Verify a DLP or comprehensive audit logging solution is deployed to monitor information transfers — requires manual verification

checks.name: USB Storage Module is Blacklisted
checks.purpose: Verify the usb-storage kernel module is blacklisted in /etc/modprobe.d/ or not currently loaded, preventing removable storage as a data transfer channel

checks.name: Outbound Firewall Rules Are Configured
checks.purpose: Verify iptables OUTPUT or firewalld rules are configured to restrict unauthorized outbound data transfer paths

checks.name: DLP or Auditing Solution Present
checks.purpose: Verify a DLP or comprehensive audit logging solution is deployed to monitor information transfers — requires manual verification

remediation: Deploy a DLP solution such as Microsoft Purview Endpoint DLP. Disable USB storage via GPO (set USBSTOR Start to 4). Configure outbound firewall rules to block unauthorized data transfer destinations. On Linux, blacklist usb-storage in /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist.conf and configure iptables OUTPUT rules.
